title.en: HR-WPAN Orientation: Scope, Purpose, Word Usage, and the Vocabulary of 802.15.3
title.zh: HR-WPAN 入门导览：范围、目的、用语约定，以及 802.15.3 的术语表

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802.15.3-2023 is the standard for high-rate wireless personal area networks (HR-WPANs) — short-range radio links built for moving real multimedia data fast. Before you can read any of its later clauses on frames, timing, or physical layers, you need three foundations: what the standard is for (Clause 1) and what its words actually mean (Clause 3). This lesson is your orientation map to both, naming the exact clause numbers as we go so you can always find your way back.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802.15.3-2023 是关于高速率无线个域网（HR-WPAN，high-rate wireless personal area network）的标准——一种为快速传输多媒体数据而设计的短距离无线链路。在阅读它后续关于帧、时序或物理层的任何条款之前，你需要先打好三块地基：这份标准是做什么用的（第 1 章），以及它的术语究竟是什么含义（第 3 章）。本课就是带你了解这两部分的导览图，沿途会标出确切的条款编号，方便你随时找回坐标。

blocks.en: Clause 1.1 (Scope) draws the boundary. The standard defines two layers: the physical layer (PHY), which actually sends bits over the air, and the medium access control (MAC) sublayer, which decides who transmits when. Its target is high data rate connectivity — typically over 200 Mb/s — across devices that are fixed, portable, or moving. The headline use cases are consumer multimedia, emerging wireless switched point-to-point links, and high-rate close-proximity point-to-point applications. If a use case is not high-rate or not short-range, it is outside this standard's scope.
blocks.zh: 第 1.1 节（范围）划定了边界。本标准定义两个层：物理层（PHY，physical layer），负责真正把比特送上空中；以及介质访问控制子层（MAC，medium access control），负责决定谁在何时发送。它的目标是高数据率连接——通常超过 200 Mb/s——覆盖固定、便携或移动的设备。核心应用场景包括消费类多媒体、新兴的无线交换式点对点链路，以及高速率近距离点对点应用。如果某个场景既不是高速率也不是短距离，那它就落在本标准的范围之外。

blocks.en: Clause 1.2 (Purpose) explains the design intent behind that scope. The standard aims for low complexity, low cost, low power consumption, and high data rate all at once — a deliberately demanding combination. The named applications stretch from consumer multimedia to wireless switched point-to-point links inside data centers, wireless backhaul/fronthaul and intra-device communications, and quick close-proximity transfers such as a phone dumping a large file to a kiosk or ticket gate, or to a wireless storage device. Purpose answers why; scope answers what.
blocks.zh: 第 1.2 节（目的）解释了该范围背后的设计意图。本标准力求同时做到低复杂度、低成本、低功耗与高数据率——这是一个刻意设定的苛刻组合。其列举的应用从消费类多媒体，延伸到数据中心内部的无线交换式点对点链路、无线回传/前传与设备内通信，以及快速的近距离传输，例如手机把一个大文件转储到自助终端机或检票闸机，或转储到无线存储设备。目的回答的是“为什么”；范围回答的是“是什么”。

blocks.en: Scope vs Purpose is a recurring IEEE pattern. Scope (1.1) is the legal boundary — what the document specifies. Purpose (1.2) is the motivation — the goals and use cases that justify it. When two readers argue about whether a feature 'belongs', the answer is almost always settled by Scope, not Purpose.
blocks.zh: “范围”与“目的”是 IEEE 标准中反复出现的固定结构。范围（1.1）是法律意义上的边界——文档规定了什么。目的（1.2）是动机——为之辩护的目标与应用场景。当两位读者争论某个特性是否“属于”本标准时，答案几乎总是由范围而非目的来裁定。

blocks.en: Clause 1.3 (Word usage) is small but load-bearing: it fixes the meaning of the standard's verbs. shall means a mandatory requirement (is required to). should means recommended but not required (is recommended that). may means permitted (is permitted to). can means a statement of possibility or capability (is able to). Two words are deprecated for requirements: must (only for unavoidable situations) and will (only for statements of fact). Misreading a should as a shall is one of the most common mistakes new readers make.
blocks.zh: 第 1.3 节（用语约定）篇幅虽小却至关重要：它固定了本标准中各动词的含义。shall 表示强制性要求（“是必须做到的”）。should 表示推荐但非强制（“是建议这样做的”）。may 表示允许（“是被准许这样做的”）。can 用于陈述可能性或能力（“是能够做到的”）。有两个词在表述要求时已被弃用：must（仅用于不可避免的情形）与 will（仅用于陈述事实）。把 should 误读成 shall，是新读者最常犯的错误之一。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 420 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"><text x="10" y="20" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="13">Word usage (1.3) — conformance verbs</text><rect x="10" y="35" width="95" height="40"/><text x="57" y="55" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">shall</text><text x="57" y="69" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">required</text><rect x="115" y="35" width="95" height="40"/><text x="162" y="55" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">should</text><text x="162" y="69" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">recommended</text><rect x="220" y="35" width="95" height="40"/><text x="267" y="55" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">may</text><text x="267" y="69" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">permitted</text><rect x="325" y="35" width="85" height="40"/><text x="367" y="55" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">can</text><text x="367" y="69" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">able to</text><text x="10" y="105" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11">deprecated for requirements:</text><rect x="10" y="115" width="95" height="28" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="57" y="133" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">must</text><rect x="115" y="115" width="95" height="28" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="162" y="133" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">will</text></svg>

caption.en: The four conformance verbs of Clause 1.3, with the two deprecated words shown dashed.
caption.zh: 第 1.3 节的四个一致性动词，两个被弃用的词以虚线框表示。

blocks.en: Clause 3.1 (Definitions) is where the standard's nouns get pinned down. A few are the spine of everything that follows. A device (DEV) is any entity implementing a conformant MAC and PHY interface to the wireless medium. A piconet is a collection of logically associated devices sharing one identifier under a common coordinator — the piconet coordinator (PNC), which provides coordination, QoS (quality of service), synchronization, and association. A pairnet is the minimal case: at most two associated devices, one acting as pairnet coordinator (PRC). Association is the service that assigns a device identifier so a DEV can communicate in a piconet; disassociation removes it.
blocks.zh: 第 3.1 节（定义）是把本标准中各名词钉死含义的地方。其中几个是后续一切内容的脊梁。设备（DEV，device）是指任何实现了符合本标准的 MAC 与 PHY 接口、可接入无线介质的实体。微微网（piconet）是一组逻辑上相互关联、共享同一标识符、并处于同一协调者之下的设备——这个协调者就是微微网协调器（PNC，piconet coordinator），它负责提供协调、QoS（quality of service，服务质量）、同步与关联。配对网（pairnet）是最小化的情形：至多两个相互关联的设备，其中一个充当配对网协调器（PRC，pairnet coordinator）。关联（association）是为设备分配标识符、使其能够在微微网内通信的服务；解除关联（disassociation）则移除该标识符。

blocks.en: Two more definition families matter early. Timing: a superframe is the basic time division of a piconet, containing a beacon, the channel time allocation period, and optionally a contention access period; a channel time allocation (CTA) is a contiguous slice of that superframe the PNC reserves between a specified source and destination. Data units: the MAC service data unit (MSDU) is what arrives from above, the MAC protocol data unit (MPDU) is what two MAC peers exchange, and the MAC command data unit (MCDU) carries management protocol commands. A stream is a unidirectional logical connection, possibly carrying QoS requirements.
blocks.zh: 还有两组定义在早期就很重要。时序方面：超帧（superframe）是微微网的基本时间划分单元，包含一个信标（beacon）、信道时间分配周期，以及可选的竞争访问周期；信道时间分配（CTA，channel time allocation）则是 PNC 在指定源与目的之间预留的、超帧中一段连续的时间片。数据单元方面：MAC 服务数据单元（MSDU，MAC service data unit）是从上层送达的数据，MAC 协议数据单元（MPDU，MAC protocol data unit）是两个 MAC 对等实体之间交换的单元，而 MAC 命令数据单元（MCDU，MAC command data unit）承载管理协议命令。流（stream）是一条单向的逻辑连接，可能附带 QoS 要求。

blocks.en:
flowchart TD
  PNC["PNC: piconet coordinator"] -->|beacon + CTAs| DEV1["DEV (device)"]
  PNC -->|association| DEV2["DEV (device)"]
  DEV1 -->|stream over a CTA| DEV2
  subgraph SF["superframe"]
    B["beacon"] --> CTAP["CTA period"] --> CAP["contention access period (optional)"]
  end
  PNC --- SF
blocks.zh:
flowchart TD
  PNC["PNC：微微网协调器"] -->|信标 + CTA| DEV1["DEV（设备）"]
  PNC -->|关联| DEV2["DEV（设备）"]
  DEV1 -->|经由 CTA 的流| DEV2
  subgraph SF["超帧"]
    B["信标"] --> CTAP["CTA 周期"] --> CAP["竞争访问周期（可选）"]
  end
  PNC --- SF

blocks.en: Clause 3.2 (Acronyms and abbreviations) is the standard's master glossary — every short form you will meet later is expanded here once. They cluster into themes: network roles (DEV, PNC, PRC, DME — device management entity; MLME — MAC layer management entity; PLME — PHY layer management entity); identifiers (DEVID, PNID, BSID — beacon source identifier, SrcID/DestID); acknowledgment styles (Imm-ACK, Dly-ACK, Blk-ACK, Imp-ACK, Stk-ACK, NAK); timing (SIFS, MIFS, RIFS, BIFS, ATP — association timeout period); and PHY/signal terms (OFDM, QAM, BPSK/QPSK, MCS — modulation and coding scheme, SNR, EIRP, FEC, LDPC, CCA — clear channel assessment).
blocks.zh: 第 3.2 节（缩略语与缩写）是本标准的主术语表——你后续会遇到的每一个缩写，都在这里被完整展开一次。它们按主题聚类：网络角色（DEV、PNC、PRC、DME——设备管理实体；MLME——MAC 层管理实体；PLME——PHY 层管理实体）；标识符（DEVID、PNID、BSID——信标源标识符、SrcID/DestID）；确认方式（Imm-ACK、Dly-ACK、Blk-ACK、Imp-ACK、Stk-ACK、NAK）；时序（SIFS、MIFS、RIFS、BIFS、ATP——关联超时周期）；以及 PHY 与信号术语（OFDM、QAM、BPSK/QPSK、MCS——调制与编码方案、SNR、EIRP、FEC、LDPC、CCA——空闲信道评估）。

blocks.en: When a later clause throws an unexplained acronym at you, do not guess — jump straight to 3.2. It is alphabetical and exhaustive. For full noun definitions (not just the expansion), use 3.1. Together, 3.1 and 3.2 are the dictionary you keep open beside every other clause.
blocks.zh: 当后续某条款抛给你一个未加解释的缩写时，不要猜——直接跳到 3.2。它按字母排序且收录完整。若需要名词的完整定义（而不仅是缩写展开），则查 3.1。3.1 与 3.2 合在一起，就是你阅读其他每一条款时都应在旁边摊开的那本词典。

summary.en: Coverage map: exactly which page teaches each unit (1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 3.1, 3.2)
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：每个单元（1.1、1.2、1.3、3.1、3.2）究竟由哪一页讲授

blocks.en: Clause 1 (Overview) lives on PDF p.34. 1.1 Scope defines PHY + MAC for high-rate (>200 Mb/s) connectivity among fixed/portable/moving devices. 1.2 Purpose lists the low-complexity/low-cost/low-power/high-rate goal and use cases (consumer multimedia, data-center point-to-point, backhaul/fronthaul, close-proximity transfers). 1.3 Word usage defines shall/should/may/can and deprecates must/will. Clause 3 (Definitions, acronyms, and abbreviations) begins on p.36. 3.1 Definitions spans p.36-37, defining terms from 'ad hoc network' and 'association' through 'device', 'piconet', 'pairnet', 'superframe', 'CTA', 'stream', the MxDU family (MSDU/MPDU/MCDU), and 'wireless medium'. 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations runs p.37-42, an alphabetical expansion list from AAS (asymmetric antenna system) through WLAN (wireless local area network). (Clause 2 Normative references, on p.35, and Clause 4 General description, starting p.43, bracket these but are taught in separate lessons.)
blocks.zh: 第 1 章（概述）位于 PDF 第 34 页。1.1 范围定义了面向固定/便携/移动设备的高速率（>200 Mb/s）连接的 PHY 与 MAC。1.2 目的列出了低复杂度/低成本/低功耗/高速率的目标及应用场景（消费类多媒体、数据中心点对点、回传/前传、近距离传输）。1.3 用语约定定义了 shall/should/may/can，并弃用 must/will。第 3 章（定义、缩略语与缩写）从第 36 页开始。3.1 定义跨第 36-37 页，从“ad hoc network（自组织网络）”“association（关联）”一直定义到“device（设备）”“piconet（微微网）”“pairnet（配对网）”“superframe（超帧）”“CTA（信道时间分配）”“stream（流）”、MxDU 家族（MSDU/MPDU/MCDU）以及“wireless medium（无线介质）”。3.2 缩略语与缩写跨第 37-42 页，是一份按字母排序的展开清单，从 AAS（非对称天线系统）到 WLAN（无线局域网）。（第 35 页的第 2 章规范性引用文件，以及从第 43 页起的第 4 章总体描述，分别在它们前后，但会在其他课中讲授。）

blocks.en: With these foundations in hand, the rest of 802.15.3 becomes readable. You know the standard's edges (1.1), its motivations (1.2), how to read its requirements precisely (1.3), and where to look up every term (3.1) and acronym (3.2). Keep Clause 3 open as your reference companion, and the dense clauses on MAC frames, superframe timing, security, and the several PHYs will read far more smoothly.
blocks.zh: 掌握了这些地基之后，802.15.3 的其余部分就变得可读了。你已经知道这份标准的边界（1.1）、它的动机（1.2）、如何精确解读它的要求（1.3），以及到哪里查阅每个术语（3.1）与每个缩写（3.2）。把第 3 章当作随身参考摊在一旁，那些关于 MAC 帧、超帧时序、安全与多种 PHY 的密集条款，读起来就会顺畅得多。

cites.note: Clause 1 Overview: 1.1 Scope, 1.2 Purpose, 1.3 Word usage (shall/should/may/can; must/will deprecated).
Clause 3.1 Definitions: device, piconet, PNC, pairnet, association, superframe, CTA, stream, MSDU/MPDU/MCDU, wireless medium.
Clause 3.2 Acronyms and abbreviations: alphabetical glossary from AAS through WLAN.
